title: default-env.sh NO_PROXY Syntax error
body:
### Issue Description

podman machine ssh NO_PROXY env error (on macos m1pro)
```
-bash: export: `host.containers.internal,': not a valid identifier
-bash: export: `::1': not a valid identifier
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install podman>=5.8.0 via homebrew
2. podman machine init
3. podman machine ssh


### Describe the results you received

```bash
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 43.20260217.3.1
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Fri Mar 13 11:47:31 2026 from 192.168.127.1
-bash: export: `host.containers.internal,': not a valid identifier
-bash: export: `::1': not a valid identifier
core@localhost:~$ echo $NO_PROXY
host.containers.internal,
core@localhost:~$ cat /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh
export NO_PROXY=host.containers.internal, host.containers.internal, ::1
core@localhost:~$ source /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh 
-bash: export: `host.containers.internal,': not a valid identifier
-bash: export: `::1': not a valid identifier
```

### Describe the results you expected

correct NO_PROXY

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

manually install [podman 5.7.1](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.7.1/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg) with podman-machine-os 5.8.1, everything is ok.

comments.body:
I wasn't able to reproduce this error on version `5.8.1`. Could you provide more information? Also, please keep in mind that we don’t officially support Homebrew.

```bash
$ podman machine start test
Starting machine "test"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

	podman machine set --rootful test

API forwarding listening on: /var/run/docker.sock
Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.

Machine "test" started successfully
$ podman machine ssh test
Connecting to vm test. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 43.20260217.3.1
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

core@localhost:~$ echo $NO_PROXY

core@localhost:~$ cat /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh
cat: /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh: No such file or directory
core@localhost:~$

```

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

I have reset all machines with homebrew version podman. And then removed podman podman-compose installed with homebrew. Remove ~/.config/containers. **Just reset everything, then try official releases.**
```bash
podman machine reset
brew uninstall podman podman-machine
rm -rf ~/.config/containers
```

---
Macbook Pro 14 2021, m1pro, Sequoia 15.7.4

OS Image: `https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/releases/download/v5.8.1/podman-machine.aarch64.applehv.raw.zst`, save to `~/Downloads/podman-machine.aarch64.applehv.raw.zst`

In summary:
1. podman 5.7.1 new machine => ok
2. podman 5.8.1 with 5.7.1 machine => err
3. podman 5.8.1 with new machine => err
4. podman 5.7.1 with 5.8.1 machine (the first start) => err
5. podman 5.7.1 with 5.8.1 machine (next starts) => ok

---
## Run podman 5.7.1

1. install 5.7.1 by `https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.7.1/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg`

# Run podman 5.8.1

1. stop the machine, remove every installed 5.7.1 thing with UninstallPKG. machine was kept (created by 5.7.1)
2. install 5.8.1 by `https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.8.1/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg`
same steps. `NO_PROXY` error again

So I guess there maybe something to do with 5.8
Well, what's your `$NO_PROXY` value on the host? That’s the main missing piece here.

The issue lies in the content of `NO_PROXY`. Based on [this issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7531) and the [GNU documentation](https://www.gnu.org/software/wget/manual/html_node/Proxies.html), the variable should contain a comma-separated list, not a comma-plus-space-separated list.

Here are the steps I did with podman v5.8.1.
```bash
export NO_PROXY="host.containers.internal,host.containers.internal,::1"
$ podman machine init test2
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.8 to create VM
Extracting compressed file: test2-arm64.raw: done
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start test2

$ podman machine start test2
Starting machine "test2"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

	podman machine set --rootful test2

API forwarding listening on: /var/run/docker.sock
Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.

Machine "test2" started successfully
$ podman machine ssh test2
Connecting to vm test2. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 43.20260217.3.1
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

core@localhost:~$ echo $NO_PROXY
host.containers.internal,host.containers.internal,::1
core@localhost:~$ cat /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh
export NO_PROXY=host.containers.internal,host.containers.internal,::1
core@localhost:~$
```

> Well, what's your `$NO_PROXY` value on the host? That’s the main missing piece here.

`localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1`
